observations.what: Entire site framed as an 'Official Travel Guide' to Montréal — every product-demo section (weights, ligatures, language support) reskinned as travel media: boarding pass, postcard, stamp, departure board.
observations.why: Gives an otherwise dry subject an emotional throughline, making the page memorable instead of a checklist.

observations.what: The circular 'NM' logomark doubles as a loading spinner on entry and later stands in for the letter 'o' in body copy.
observations.why: Ties the brand mark directly into the typographic argument of the site instead of being purely decorative.

observations.what: A full-bleed pinned background video plays while foreground headline text scrubs continuously through the entire weight axis (Black to Hairline) tied precisely to scroll position.
observations.why: Demonstrates a variable font's actual range better than a static weight chart — the product feature IS the demo mechanism. Reads as continuous, not stepped, because it's well executed.

observations.what: A giant single glyph fills the viewport, swapped for other glyphs as you scroll, split top/bottom for Display vs Text weight comparison at huge scale.
observations.why: Lets you inspect letterforms like a monument; trades efficiency for spectacle deliberately.

observations.what: A year-by-year evolution timeline pins years in a sticky left column while a scattered, rotated collage of stamps/postcards drifts and reorders in the main viewport (a 'corkboard' effect).
observations.why: A tactile, memorable way to present chronology instead of a static timeline graphic.

observations.what: Near the end, three posters fan out and overlap like a hand of cards.
observations.why: Mirrors physical card-stacking — same underlying metaphor as Bucks Sauce's card-stack-deal, different execution.

observations.what: A persistent pill-shaped nav anchors to in-page sections; a single CTA stays fixed and never disappears across the ~22,000px page.
observations.why: One conversion point, always available, protects against the page's own indulgence in long-form storytelling.

observations.what: Motion relies almost entirely on scroll-linked (scrubbed) animation rather than autoplaying loops.
observations.why: Deliberate, disciplined — never nags for attention; pacing stays entirely under the visitor's control. Note: heavy scroll-linked reveal is historically poor for screen readers/reduced-motion unless carefully guarded, and bespoke-per-section animation with no shared component system is expensive to maintain.

throughline: Discipline through restraint of interaction, not restraint of scale — every individual moment is visually maximal, but nothing moves without the visitor's own scroll input. That's what actually reads as calm and premium, not the color or scale choices themselves.
